commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared event-session routing helper and applies it to exec, cron, node-event, heartbeat, ACP parent-stream, and CLI watchdog enqueue/wake paths so single-owner `dmScope=main` direct-DM events route to agent main.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible on current main: the enqueue helper only remaps cron-run keys and heartbeat forced-session resolution returns same-agent direct keys unchanged. The linked issue also includes production session-store evidence for the orphan-session behavior.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor proof and maintainer risk acceptance for session-routing behavior, not a narrow code repair ClawSweeper should attempt.

target.title: fix(core): route dmScope main event wakes to agent main
target.body:
Fixes #71581.
Replaces #72648 with a narrower implementation.

## Problem
When `session.dmScope` is set to `main`, OpenClaw can still receive internal exec/cron event wakes carrying an old direct-DM session key such as `agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123`. Those events are enqueued and woken on a queue the main heartbeat no longer drains, so completion notifications can sit unseen until another action happens to wake the right session.

## Change and value
This PR adds one shared event-session routing helper and uses it anywhere internal exec/cron event enqueue or heartbeat wake targets are resolved. Single-owner `dmScope=main` direct-DM event keys now route back to the drained agent main session, while existing cron-run remapping and explicit route-binding direct-session overrides remain intact.

## Who is affected, and who is not
- Affected: operators using `session.dmScope=main` with a single static DM owner allowlist, especially exec completion and cron-event wake flows.
- Unchanged: multi-owner allowlists, wildcard allowlists, non-agent node fallback sessions, route-binding direct-session overrides, and existing cron-run global/main remapping behavior.

## Why now
The original fix attempt in #72648 has stalled, but #71581 is still open and the current routing behavior still leaves a real wake/enqueue mismatch for main-scoped DM sessions. This replacement keeps the fix narrower and adds regression coverage around the shared routing boundary.

## Implementation
- Add `src/infra/event-session-routing.ts` for resolving event enqueue keys and heartbeat wake options from the same policy.
- Apply the helper to exec completion events, gateway node exec events, cron forced-session resolution, heartbeat forced-session resolution, ACP parent-stream events, and CLI watchdog events.
- Cover the new behavior in routing, exec runtime, heartbeat, and node event tests.

## Duplicate check
Before opening this PR, I searched open PRs for `71581 dmScope exec-event cron-event heartbeat`, `session.dmScope heartbeat`, and `resolveHeartbeatSession resolveEventSessionKey scopedHeartbeatWakeOptions`. No active duplicate appeared other than the abandoned #72648.

## Verification
- Local: `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts`
- Local: `pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts`
- Local: `git diff --check`
- Remote GCP Crabbox Spot VM, `e2-standard-4`, `us-east1-b`, commit `8a2a08a47f`:

```sh
OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_PREPARE=1 pnpm check:changed -- --timed
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts \
  src/infra/event-session-routing.test.ts \
  src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts \
  src/infra/heartbeat-runner.subagent-session-guard.test.ts \
  --reporter=verbose
node --import tsx -e "import { resolveEventSessionRoutingPolicy, resolveEventSessionKeyForPolicy, scopedHeartbeatWakeOptionsForPolicy } from './src/infra/event-session-routing.ts'; const cfg={session:{dmScope:'main'},channels:{telegram:{allowFrom:['123']}}}; const policy=resolveEventSessionRoutingPolicy({cfg,sessionKey:'agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123'}); if (resolveEventSessionKeyForPolicy('agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123', policy) !== 'agent:main:main') throw new Error('event key did not route to main'); const wake=scopedHeartbeatWakeOptionsForPolicy('agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123',{reason:'exec-event'},policy); if (wake.sessionKey !== 'agent:main:main') throw new Error('wake did not route to main'); console.log('event-session-routing smoke ok');"
```

Remote result: `check:changed` clean; targeted Vitest `5 passed`, `126 passed`; runtime smoke printed `event-session-routing smoke ok`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed:
- With `session.dmScope=main` and a single static DM owner allowlist, internal exec/cron event wakes can carry an old direct DM session key such as `agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123`. Those events need to enqueue and wake the agent main session that the heartbeat actually drains.

Real environment tested:
- GCP Crabbox Spot VM, `e2-standard-4`, `us-east1-b`
- Node `24.15.0`, pnpm `11.1.0`
- Commit `8a2a08a47f`

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```sh
node --import tsx -e "import { resolveEventSessionRoutingPolicy, resolveEventSessionKeyForPolicy, scopedHeartbeatWakeOptionsForPolicy } from './src/infra/event-session-routing.ts'; const cfg={session:{dmScope:'main'},channels:{telegram:{allowFrom:['123']}}}; const policy=resolveEventSessionRoutingPolicy({cfg,sessionKey:'agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123'}); if (resolveEventSessionKeyForPolicy('agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123', policy) !== 'agent:main:main') throw new Error('event key did not route to main'); const wake=scopedHeartbeatWakeOptionsForPolicy('agent:main:telegram:default:direct:123',{reason:'exec-event'},policy); if (wake.sessionKey !== 'agent:main:main') throw new Error('wake did not route to main'); console.log('event-session-routing smoke ok');"
```

Evidence after fix:
Copied live console output transcript from the GCP Crabbox shell at commit `8a2a08a47f`:
```text
v24.15.0
11.1.0
event-session-routing smoke ok
```

Observed result after fix:
- The direct-DM event key resolved to `agent:main:main` for event enqueue.
- The heartbeat wake target resolved to `agent:main:main`.
- The command printed `event-session-routing smoke ok` only after both runtime assertions passed.

What was not tested:
- A full local WSL Vitest run; local Vitest stalled before output on this host, so validation was run remotely.
- `pnpm check:changed` without `OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_PREPARE=1`; the raw VM previously hung in unrelated plugin SDK boundary-artifact lint preparation, so the final remote gate skipped only that preparation step while still running full core lint.
